purpose: Restrict fundamental rights for social welfare
text: Constitutional Amendment 1 (1951). Added Articles 31A, 31B and Ninth Schedule. Allowed state to make laws on land reform, nationalization without judicial review. Restricted freedom of speech for public order. First step toward social welfare legislation.

Constitutional Amendment 42 (1976). Called 'Mini Constitution.' Passed during Emergency. Added 'Socialist, Secular' to Preamble. Added Fundamental Duties (Part IVA). Curtailed judicial review. Made DPSPs superior to Fundamental Rights. Most extensive single amendment.

purpose: Reversed many Emergency-era changes of 42nd Amendment
text: Constitutional Amendment 44 (1978). Reversed key provisions of 42nd Amendment. Restored habeas corpus during Emergency. Right to Property removed as Fundamental Right (made legal right under Art 300A). Strengthened safeguards against misuse of Emergency provisions.

purpose: Anti-defection — prevents legislators from switching parties
text: Constitutional Amendment 52 (1985). Added Tenth Schedule — Anti-Defection Law. Disqualification of legislators who switch parties after election. Speaker's decision is final but now subject to judicial review. Prevents horse-trading and floor-crossing.

purpose: Reduced voting age from 21 to 18 years
text: Constitutional Amendment 61 (1988). Reduced voting age from 21 to 18 years by amending Article 326. Added approximately 50 million new voters. Recognized youth's role in democracy. Effective from 28 March 1989 elections.

purpose: Constitutional status to Panchayati Raj institutions
text: Constitutional Amendment 73 (1992). Gave constitutional status to Panchayati Raj. Added Part IX. Three-tier system: Gram Panchayat, Panchayat Samiti, Zila Parishad. Reserved 1/3 seats for women. SC/ST reservation. 5-year term. State Election Commission.

purpose: Constitutional status to Urban Local Bodies (municipalities)
text: Constitutional Amendment 74 (1992). Constitutional status to Urban Local Bodies. Added Part IXA. Three types: Nagar Panchayat, Municipal Council, Municipal Corporation. Reserved 1/3 seats for women. Ward committees mandatory. State Finance Commission.

purpose: Right to Education made Fundamental Right
text: Constitutional Amendment 86 (2002). Made Right to Education a Fundamental Right under Article 21A. Free and compulsory education for children aged 6-14. Added as Fundamental Duty for parents to provide education. Implemented through RTE Act 2009.

Constitutional Amendment 101 (2016). Introduced GST — One Nation One Tax. Added Articles 246A, 269A, 279A. Created GST Council (Article 279A). Subsumed 17 central and state taxes. Implemented July 1, 2017. Biggest indirect tax reform since independence.

purpose: 10% EWS reservation in education and jobs
text: Constitutional Amendment 103 (2019). 10% reservation for Economically Weaker Sections (EWS) among unreserved categories. Added Articles 15(6) and 16(6). For families with income below ₹8 lakh/year. Upheld by Supreme Court in Janhit Abhiyan case (3:2 verdict, Nov 2022).

purpose: Extended SC/ST reservation in Lok Sabha and State Assemblies by 10 years
text: Constitutional Amendment 104 (2020). Extended reservation of seats for SC/ST in Lok Sabha and State Legislative Assemblies for 10 more years (until January 2030). Original reservation was for 10 years from 1950, extended every decade since.

purpose: Women's Reservation — 33% seats in Lok Sabha and State Assemblies
text: Constitutional Amendment 106 (2023). Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam. Reserves 33% seats for women in Lok Sabha and State Legislative Assemblies. Sub-reservation for SC/ST women. Effective after delimitation exercise and census. Passed in Special Session in new Parliament.
